cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user provided a passage about the collapse of a wild salmon run and four possible statements. I need to determine which one is true based on the passage. First, I'll go through each option one by one. Starting with option A: "Last fall, there was the second lowest number of grown salmon returning on record." The passage mentions that "only about 90,000 adult salmon returned to the Central Valley last fall--the second lowest number on record..." So this seems correct. The number is the second lowest, so A is likely the right answer, but I need to check the others to be sure. Option B: "The salmon migrates to the ocean when they grow up." The passage states that wild salmon "are born in streams and rivers, migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles..." So they migrate when they're juveniles, not when they grow up. So B is incorrect. Option C: "The farm-raised salmon is not as delicious and nutrients as the wild salmon." The passage mentions that there will be abundant supplies of farm-raised salmon, but it doesn't compare their taste or nutrition to wild salmon.
